title: Herdr cockpit contract and default workspace
goal: Introduce Herdr as the optional-default live observability cockpit for Khazad-Doom runs through a small daemon-owned cockpit seam: Khazad-Doom opens/focuses Herdr workspaces and read-only feed/phase panes when available, but direct Pi execution remains the fallback and source of truth stays in daemon state.
acceptance: A small Cockpit/CockpitAdapter seam exists for opening or focusing a run workspace and creating named read-only panes; callers do not learn Herdr command details, pane ids beyond opaque references, or terminal-layout mechanics.

The default cockpit mode is auto: when a usable herdr binary is found, a run attempts to create or focus a workspace named for the run and opens a Run Status / Event Feed pane plus a read-only Integration Gate / Repair phase pane; when Herdr is unavailable or fails, the run falls back to current direct execution.
Operators can override cockpit behavior through durable config and per-run CLI flag values equivalent to auto, herdr, or direct.

Herdr cockpit failures are recorded as non-fatal cockpit-unavailable incidents or feed entries with remediation, and they never change slice selection, worker authorization, verification, merge, handoff, or terminal run status by themselves.
The Planner Pi pane is explicitly deferred until RPL planner behavior exists; HERDR-01 does not launch a planner agent.
Real-Herdr smoke evidence proves workspace creation/focus and feed/phase pane creation on an installed Herdr, while the normal full test suite remains portable when Herdr is absent.
must_ask_if: The implementation would block normal runs solely because Herdr is missing or a workspace cannot be created.
The implementation would make Herdr own workflow truth, cancellation, authorization, verification, merge, or handoff decisions.
The implementation would parse terminal text or Herdr UI state as the source of daemon truth.
The implementation would add a planner Pi session before RPL slices define planner authority.
The implementation would add a broad UI framework or full Pi monitor replacement instead of a small cockpit adapter seam.
